message.content:
Read-only scout of a bash codebase. Working dir: /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster. Do NOT edit anything. Report conclusions as `file_path:line_number` plus a one-line explanation each.

Context: We are retiring the "enshrine" base-image-mirror operation. I need the FULL set of *functional* (code) consumers of enshrine and its GAR namespace, so I can plan deletion order (delete-old-last). IMPORTANT: filter out noise — comments, doc/spec prose, and `.adoc` examples are NOT functional consumers. I want live bash code paths.

Search these signals across `Tools/rbk/` and `tt/`:
- `RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_ENSHRINES` (the functional namespace constant — find its definition and every code use)
- `rbi_es` (the literal GAR category string — note which hits are code vs comment/doc)
- functional `enshrine` uses in `.sh` files (function calls, dispatch table entries, colophon routing) — distinguish from the verb appearing in comments/strings
- `DirectorAuditsEnshrinements` / `rbw-iae`, `DirectorWrestsEnshrinedImage` / `rbw-iwe`, `DirectorJettisonsEnshrinement` / `rbw-iJe` (the image-family maintenance verbs that address the enshrine namespace — `rbfl*`/`rbgl*`)
- where `rbge`/`rbga`/`rbgl` (GAR layout) maps the ENSHRINES category

Report:
1. Definition site of `RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_ENSHRINES` and `rbi_es`.
2. Every functional CODE consumer (file:line + the function/dispatch context), grouped: (a) the writer path, (b) maintenance verbs (audit/wrest/jettison enshrinement), (c) any GAR-layout helpers keyed on the enshrines category.
3. Explicitly flag which hits are comment/doc-only noise (one line, so I know they're excluded).

Tight. I need the deletion-order dependency picture, not a file dump.

- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- rbk-wrangle: Muster, then roster RBK and JJK heats as two space-padded plain-text blocks
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at

15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle GCP Constants - Implementation (no printf required)
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	# Multiple inclusion detection
24	# (Module state remains ZRBGC_* per BCG; external constants use RBGC_*)
25	test -z "${ZRBGC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
26	ZRBGC_SOURCED=1
27	
28	# Tinder constants (pure string literals, no variable expansion — available at source time)
29	# Depot project ID infix between RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX and RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER, consumed
30	# by rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh's RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID derivation.
31	RBGC_depot_project_infix="d-"
32	
33	######################################################################
34	# Internal Functions (zrbgc_*)
35	
36	zrbgc_kindle() {
37	  test -z "${ZRBGC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgc already kindled"
38	
39	  # Global Resource Naming (Google Cloud global namespace)
40	  # These resources compete in globally-unique namespaces across all of GCP
41	  # Pattern: {prefix}-{type}-{name}-{timestamp} where timestamp is YYMMDDHHMMSS
42	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX="rbwg"
43	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_PAYOR="p"
44	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT="d"
45	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_BUCKET="b"
46	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_FORMAT="+%y%m%d%H%M%S"
47	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN=12
48	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX="[0-9]{${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN}}"
49	
50	  # Global resource validation patterns
51	  # Payor:  rbwg-p-YYMMDDHHMMSS  (timestamp survives — payor is installation-scoped, not depot-scoped)
52	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_PAYOR_REGEX="^${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_PAYOR}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX}$"
53	
54	  # Basic Configuration
55	  readonly RBGC_ADMIN_ROLE="rbw-admin"
56	  readonly RBGC_PAYOR_ROLE="rbw-payor"
57	  readonly RBGC_PAYOR_APP_NAME="Recipe Bottle Payor"
58	
59	  # Timeouts
60	  readonly RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC=90
61	  readonly RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC=3
62	  # Consecutive 404s rbuh_poll_until_gone requires before declaring a deleted
63	  # resource durably gone. GCP IAM's SA read path is multi-replica eventually-
64	  # consistent: a post-DELETE GET flaps 200<->404 for seconds as replicas
65	  # converge, so a single 404 is not durable proof — a same-name recreate can
66	  # still race a lagging replica's existence preflight. The streak debounces
67	  # that flap; any intervening 200 resets it. Bounded by RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC.
68	  readonly RBGC_GONE_CONFIRM_STREAK=3
69	  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX=7
70	  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC=10
71	
72	  # JWT-bearer consumer-side retry — every RBRA consumer absorbs the post-write
73	  # race where Google's OAuth backend has not yet accepted a freshly-minted SA
74	  # or its key, surfaced as `invalid_grant` paired with either
75	  # `Invalid JWT Signature.` (fresh-key lag) or `Invalid grant: account not
76	  # found` (fresh-SA lag). 90s budget locked by ₣BB pristine-tier contract;
77	  # cadence below.
78	  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_BUDGET_SEC=90
79	  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC=2
80	  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_MAX_DELAY_SEC=15
81	
82	  # IAM-grant propagation retry — exponential-backoff budget shared by every
83	  # get-modify-set IAM grant site in rbgi_IAM.sh plus the inline GAR retry in
84	  # rbgg_invest_director. Recognizes three propagation classes against the
85	  # same time budget: (A) forward member-visibility (HTTP 400 "does not
86	  # exist"), (B) backward member-visibility (HTTP 400 "is not deleted"),
87	  # (C) caller-recently-empowered (HTTP 403 from resource-scope IAM caches).
88	  # Class C is time-bounded only — real propagation succeeds within budget,
89	  # real denial waits the budget and fails cleanly. RBSCIP locks the profile.
90	  readonly RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC=3
91	  readonly RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC=20
92	  readonly RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC=420
93	
94	  # HTTP transient-failure retry — bounded retry on curl-network blips
95	  # (connection refused 7, timeout 28, TLS handshake 35, recv failure 56).
96	  # Shared by rbuh_json and the OAuth token-mint POST. Other curl exits
97	  # are configuration-deterministic and fail fast.
98	  readonly RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS=3
99	  readonly RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_SLEEP_SEC=3
100

101	# Verify reliquary tool images exist in GAR.
102	# Full-subtree presence check: every canonical tool image must be present at
103	# the stamp. Inscribe creates all 6 atomically; piecemeal jettison or registry
104	# damage compromises the stamp's integrity. Misses are accumulated across the
105	# whole tool set so the operator sees full damage in one report.
106	# Args: token vessel_dir
107	zrbfd_preflight_reliquary() {
108	  zrbfd_sentinel
109	
110	  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
111	  local -r z_vessel_dir="${2:-}"
112	  test -n "${z_token}"      || buc_die "zrbfd_preflight_reliquary: token required"
113	  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "zrbfd_preflight_reliquary: vessel_dir required"
114	
115	  local -r z_reliquary="${RBRV_RELIQUARY:-}"
116	  test -n "${z_reliquary}" || buc_die "RBRV_RELIQUARY required on every ordain-path vessel — yoke a reliquary stamp via tt/${RBZ_YOKE_RELIQUARY}.sh before ordaining"
117	
118	  buc_step "Verifying reliquary tool images exist in GAR"
119	
120	  local -r z_canonical_tools=(
121	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCLOUD}"
122	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_DOCKER}"
123	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_ALPINE}"
124	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SYFT}"
125	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_BINFMT}"
126	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SKOPEO}"
127	  )
128	
129	  local z_missing=()
130	  local z_tool=""
131	  local z_pkg=""
132	  local z_status_file=""
133	  local z_response_file=""
134	  local z_stderr_file=""
135	  local z_http_code=""
136	
137	  for z_tool in "${z_canonical_tools[@]}"; do
138	    z_pkg="${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${z_reliquary}/${z_tool}"
139	    z_status_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}reliquary_${z_tool}_status.txt"
140	    z_response_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}reliquary_${z_tool}_response.txt"
141	    z_stderr_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}reliquary_${z_tool}_stderr.txt"
142	
143	    curl --head -sS \
144	      --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
145	      --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}" \
146	      -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}" \
147	      -H "Accept: ${ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES}" \
148	      -w "%{http_code}" \
149	      -o "${z_response_file}" \
150	      "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_pkg}/manifests/${z_reliquary}" \
151	      > "${z_status_file}" 2>"${z_stderr_file}" \
152	      || buc_die "HEAD request failed for reliquary tool: ${z_pkg}:${z_reliquary} — see ${z_stderr_file}"
153	
154	    z_http_code=$(<"${z_status_file}")
155	    test -n "${z_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty for reliquary check: ${z_tool}"
156	
157	    case "${z_http_code}" in
158	      200) buc_log_args "Reliquary tool present: ${z_tool}" ;;
159	      404) z_missing+=("${z_tool}") ;;
160	      *)   buc_die "Unexpected HTTP ${z_http_code} when checking reliquary tool: ${z_pkg}:${z_reliquary}" ;;
161	    esac
162	  done
163	
164	  if test "${#z_missing[@]}" -eq 0; then
165	    buc_info "Reliquary verified: ${z_reliquary} (${#z_canonical_tools[@]}/${#z_canonical_tools[@]} tools present)"
166	    return 0
167	  fi
168	
169	  buc_warn "Reliquary integrity check failed: ${z_reliquary} (${#z_missing[@]}/${#z_canonical_tools[@]} tools missing)"
170	  buc_bare "  The reliquary is a co-versioned set of builder tool images (gcloud, docker,"
171	  buc_bare "  syft, alpine, binfmt, skopeo) inscribed from upstream into your private GAR."
172	  buc_bare "  Air-gapped worker pools cannot pull from the public internet — the reliquary"
173	  buc_bare "  stages these tools so builds can run without egress. Piecemeal jettison is"
174	  buc_bare "  allowed but unrecoverable surgically: re-inscribe the whole stamp."
175	  buc_bare ""
176	  for z_tool in "${z_missing[@]}"; do
177	    buc_bare "  PRECHECK: GAR image not found at ${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${z_reliquary}/${z_tool}"
178	    buc_bare "    Required by ${RBRV_SIGIL}'s RBRV_RELIQUARY=${z_reliquary}."
179	  done
180	  buc_bare ""
181	  buc_bare "  Recover by re-inscribing the reliquary, then re-yoking and re-ordaining:"
182	  buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_INSCRIBE_RELIQUARY}"
183	  buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_YOKE_RELIQUARY}" "<new-stamp>"
184	  buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ORDAIN_HALLMARK}" "${z_vessel_dir}"
185	  buc_die "Registry preflight failed — ${#z_missing[@]} of ${#z_canonical_tools[@]} reliquary tool images missing from GAR"

890	  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path) and load
891	  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
892	  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
893	  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"
894	  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"
895	  test "${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE:-}" = "rbnve_conjure" \
896	    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' is not a conjure vessel (mode: ${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE:-unset})"
897	
898	  # Check at least one slot declares an upstream ORIGIN. ANCHOR is a derived
899	  # value computed by this operation from ORIGIN; a vessel with no ORIGIN has
900	  # nothing for enshrine to mirror.
901	  local z_has_origin=false
902	  local z_n=""
903	  local z_origin_var=""
904	  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
905	    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ORIGIN"
906	    test -z "${!z_origin_var:-}" || z_has_origin=true
907	  done
908	  test "${z_has_origin}" = "true" \
909	    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' declares no upstream base-image slots (RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN)"
910	
911	  # Resolve tool images from reliquary (enshrine uses skopeo from reliquary)
912	  zrbfc_resolve_tool_images
913	
914	  # Authenticate as Director
915	  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
916	  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"
917	
918	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
919	  local z_token=""
920	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
921	    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"
922	
923	  # Verify reliquary tool images exist before submitting
924	  zrbfd_preflight_reliquary "${z_token}" "${z_vessel_dir}"
925	
926	  # Always submit the Cloud Build job. The CB step inspects current upstream
927	  # for each non-empty ORIGIN slot, computes the content-addressed locator
928	  # from the live manifest digest, and copies into GAR. Extract overwrites
929	  # whatever was previously committed in RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR with the just-
930	  # computed value — the ANCHOR is a derived artifact of the current upstream,
931	  # not a pin.
932	  zrbfd_enshrine_submit "${z_token}"
933	
934	  local -r z_rbrv_file="${z_vessel_dir}/rbrv.env"
935	  zrbfd_enshrine_extract_anchors "${z_rbrv_file}"
936	
937	  buc_success "Enshrine complete for vessel: ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
938	}
939	
940	# Internal: Submit enshrine Cloud Build job
941	# Single step: skopeo inspect + copy for each ORIGIN slot, returning anchors via buildStepOutputs.
942	zrbfd_enshrine_submit() {
943	  zrbfd_sentinel
944	
945	  local -r z_token="$1"
946	
947	  buc_step "Constructing enshrine Cloud Build resource"
948	  local -r z_gar_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
949	  local -r z_gar_path="${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
950	  local -r z_mason_sa="projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/serviceAccounts/${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}"
951	
952	  # Assemble enshrine step from script
953	  local -r z_script_path="${ZRBFD_RBGJE_STEPS_DIR}/rbgje01-enshrine-copy.sh"
954	  test -f "${z_script_path}" || buc_die "Enshrine step script not found: ${z_script_path}"
955	
956	  local -r z_body_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}body.txt"
957	  local -r z_escaped_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}escaped.txt"
958	
959	  buc_log_args "Reading enshrine step script (skip shebang)"
960	  zrbfc_write_script_body "${z_script_path}" "${z_body_file}" \
961	    || buc_die "Failed to read enshrine step script"
962	  local z_body=""
963	  z_body=$(<"${z_body_file}")
964	  test -n "${z_body}" || buc_die "Empty enshrine script body"
965	
966	  printf '#!/bin/bash\n%s' "${z_body}" > "${z_escaped_file}" \
967	    || buc_die "Failed to write escaped enshrine script body"
968	
969	  local -r z_step_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}step.json"
970	  echo "[]" > "${z_step_file}" || buc_die "Failed to initialize enshrine step JSON"
971	
972	  local -r z_step_built="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}step_built.json"
973	  jq \
974	    --arg name "${z_rbfc_tool_skopeo}" \
975	    --arg id "enshrine-copy" \
976	    --rawfile script "${z_escaped_file}" \
977	    '. + [{name: $name, id: $id, script: $script}]' \
978	    "${z_step_file}" > "${z_step_built}" \
979	    || buc_die "Failed to build enshrine step JSON"
980	  mv "${z_step_built}" "${z_step_file}" \
981	    || buc_die "Failed to finalize enshrine step JSON"
982	
983	  # Step 0: in-pool reliquary preflight (defense-in-depth)
984	  local -r z_preflight_step_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}preflight_step.json"
985	  zrbfc_assemble_preflight_step "${z_preflight_step_file}" "${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}"
986	
987	  local -r z_combined_steps_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}combined_steps.json"
988	  jq -s '.[0] + .[1]' "${z_preflight_step_file}" "${z_step_file}" \
989	    > "${z_combined_steps_file}" \
990	    || buc_die "Failed to combine preflight and enshrine steps"
991	
992	  # Compose Build resource JSON
993	  buc_log_args "Composing enshrine Build resource JSON"
994	  local -r z_build_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}build.json"
995	
996	  # ORIGIN substitution per slot: pass non-empty ORIGIN through verbatim; empty
997	  # slots become empty substitutions so the CB step's `test -n "${ORIGIN}"
998	  # || continue` guard skips them.
999	  local -r z_sub_origin_1="${RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN:-}"
1000	  local -r z_sub_origin_2="${RBRV_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN:-}"
1001	  local -r z_sub_origin_3="${RBRV_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN:-}"
1002	
1003	  jq -n \
1004	    --slurpfile zjq_steps        "${z_combined_steps_file}" \
1005	    --arg zjq_sa                 "${z_mason_sa}" \
1006	    --arg zjq_gar_host           "${z_gar_host}" \
1007	    --arg zjq_gar_path           "${z_gar_path}" \
1008	    --arg zjq_enshrines_root     "${RBGL_ENSHRINES_ROOT}" \
1009	    --arg zjq_enshrines_category "${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_ENSHRINES}" \
1010	    --arg zjq_origin_1           "${z_sub_origin_1}" \
1011	    --arg zjq_origin_2           "${z_sub_origin_2}" \
1012	    --arg zjq_origin_3           "${z_sub_origin_3}" \
1013	    --arg zjq_reliquaries_root   "${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}" \
1014	    --arg zjq_reliquary          "${RBRV_RELIQUARY}" \
1015	    --arg zjq_pool               "${RBDC_POOL_TETHER}" \
1016	    --arg zjq_timeout            "${RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT}" \
1017	    '{
1018	      steps: $zjq_steps[0],
1019	      substitutions: {
1020	        _RBGE_GAR_HOST:           $zjq_gar_host,
1021	        _RBGE_GAR_PATH:           $zjq_gar_path,
1022	        _RBGE_ENSHRINES_ROOT:     $zjq_enshrines_root,
1023	        _RBGE_ENSHRINES_CATEGORY: $zjq_enshrines_category,
1024	        _RBGE_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN:     $zjq_origin_1,
1025	        _RBGE_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN:     $zjq_origin_2,
1026	        _RBGE_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN:     $zjq_origin_3,
1027	        _RBGR_GAR_HOST:           $zjq_gar_host,
1028	        _RBGR_GAR_PATH:           $zjq_gar_path,
1029	        _RBGR_RELIQUARIES_ROOT:   $zjq_reliquaries_root,
1030	        _RBGR_RELIQUARY:          $zjq_reliquary,
1031	        _RBGR_ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_1: "",
1032	        _RBGR_ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_2: "",

30	
31	  buc_doc_brief "Tally hallmarks with health status (vouched / pending / incomplete)"
32	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
33	
34	  buc_step "Authenticating as Retriever"

41	  buc_step "Enumerating hallmarks under ${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/"
42	  zrbfc_list_packages_capture "${z_token}" "${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}"

44	  if ! test -s "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"; then
45	    buc_info "No hallmarks found under ${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/"
46	    buc_success "Tally complete — 0 hallmarks"
47	    return 0
48	  fi
49	
50	  # Load-then-iterate. A synthetic sentinel element appended to the array
51	  # lets the final hallmark flush through the same boundary branch as every
52	  # intermediate one (single flush site).
53	  local z_lines=()
54	  local z_line=""
55	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
56	    z_lines+=("${z_line}")
57	  done < "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"
58	  z_lines+=("__SENTINEL__ __SENTINEL__")
59	
60	  echo ""
61	  printf "  %-30s  %-11s  %s\n" "HALLMARK" "HEALTH" "BASENAMES"
62	  printf "  %-30s  %-11s  %s\n" "------------------------------" "-----------" "---------"

66	  # restoration via about/vouch metadata is AAL territory.
67	  local z_prev_h="" z_prev_bns=""

69	  local z_count=0 z_vouched_n=0 z_pending_n=0 z_incomplete_n=0
70	  local z_i="" z_h="" z_b="" z_health=""
71	
72	  for z_i in "${!z_lines[@]}"; do
73	    z_line="${z_lines[$z_i]}"
74	    test -n "${z_line}" || continue
75	
76	    z_h="${z_line%% *}"
77	    z_b="${z_line#* }"
78	    test -n "${z_h}" || continue
79	    test -n "${z_b}" || continue
80	
81	    if test "${z_h}" != "${z_prev_h}"; then
82	      if test -n "${z_prev_h}"; then
83	        if test "${z_prev_img}" = "1" \
84	          && test "${z_prev_abt}" = "1" \
85	          && test "${z_prev_vch}" = "1"; then
86	          z_health="vouched"
87	          z_vouched_n=$(( z_vouched_n + 1 ))

90	          z_health="pending"
91	          z_pending_n=$(( z_pending_n + 1 ))
92	        else
93	          z_health="incomplete"
94	          z_incomplete_n=$(( z_incomplete_n + 1 ))
95	        fi
96	        printf "  %-30s  %-11s  %s\n" "${z_prev_h}" "${z_health}" "${z_prev_bns}"
97	        z_count=$(( z_count + 1 ))
98	      fi
99	
100	      case "${z_h}" in
101	        __SENTINEL__) break ;;
102	      esac
103	
104	      z_prev_h="${z_h}"

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJE Step 01: Enshrine upstream base images to GAR via skopeo
3	# Builder: skopeo (from reliquary)
4	# Substitutions: _RBGE_GAR_HOST, _RBGE_GAR_PATH, _RBGE_ENSHRINES_ROOT,
5	#                _RBGE_ENSHRINES_CATEGORY,
6	#                _RBGE_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN, _RBGE_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN, _RBGE_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN
7	#
8	# For each non-empty ORIGIN slot: inspect upstream manifest, compute anchor,
9	# skopeo copy --all to GAR. Write JSON locator results to /builder/outputs/output.
10	# Mason SA ambient auth via Cloud Build metadata server for GAR destination.
11	#
12	# Image URI shape: <host>/<path>/<ENSHRINES_ROOT>/<ANCHOR>:<ANCHOR>
13	# Locator shape (written back to rbrv.env): <ENSHRINES_CATEGORY>/<ANCHOR>:<ANCHOR>
14	# Anchor as both path segment and tag — each anchor is a peer package under
15	# the enshrines namespace, not a tag on a shared package.
16	
17	set -euo pipefail
18	echo "=== Enshrine base images to GAR ==="
19	
20	# Obtain OAuth2 token from metadata server (Mason SA)
21	echo "Fetching OAuth2 token from metadata server"
22	TOKEN_JSON=$(curl -sf -H "Metadata-Flavor: Google" \
23	  "http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token") \
24	  || { echo "Failed to fetch OAuth2 token from metadata server" >&2; exit 1; }
25	
26	TOKEN=$(printf '%s' "${TOKEN_JSON}" | grep -o '"access_token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
27	test -n "${TOKEN}" || { echo "Failed to extract access_token from metadata response" >&2; exit 1; }
28	
29	# CB substitutions are expanded at submit time, not available as shell variables.
30	# Capture each into a runtime variable so we can loop.
31	SLOT_1_ORIGIN="${_RBGE_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN}"
32	SLOT_2_ORIGIN="${_RBGE_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN}"
33	SLOT_3_ORIGIN="${_RBGE_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN}"
34	
35	# Initialize result JSON
36	RESULT='{'
37	FIRST=true
38	
39	for SLOT in 1 2 3; do
40	  case "${SLOT}" in
41	    1) ORIGIN="${SLOT_1_ORIGIN}" ;;
42	    2) ORIGIN="${SLOT_2_ORIGIN}" ;;
43	    3) ORIGIN="${SLOT_3_ORIGIN}" ;;
44	  esac
45	  test -n "${ORIGIN}" || continue
46	
47	  echo "--- Slot ${SLOT}: ${ORIGIN} ---"
48	
49	  # Inspect upstream for raw manifest (manifest list or single manifest)
50	  RAW_FILE="/workspace/enshrine_raw_${SLOT}.json"
51	  skopeo inspect --raw "docker://${ORIGIN}" > "${RAW_FILE}" \
52	    || { echo "FATAL: Failed to inspect upstream: ${ORIGIN}" >&2; exit 1; }
53	
54	  # Compute sha256 of the raw manifest
55	  # Note: sha256sum (coreutils), not openssl — this runs inside the skopeo reliquary container
56	  SHA=$(sha256sum "${RAW_FILE}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
57	  test -n "${SHA}" || { echo "FATAL: Empty digest for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }
58	
59	  # Construct anchor: sanitize origin (: and / become -), append first 10 hex chars
60	  SANITIZED=$(printf '%s' "${ORIGIN}" | tr ':/' '--')
61	  SHORT="${SHA:0:10}"
62	  ANCHOR="${SANITIZED}-${SHORT}"
63	
64	  # Compose locator (package-path:tag, no cloud prefix — caller prepends at use-site)
65	  LOCATOR="${_RBGE_ENSHRINES_CATEGORY}/${ANCHOR}:${ANCHOR}"
66	
67	  echo "Anchor: ${ANCHOR}"
68	  echo "Locator: ${LOCATOR}"
69	  echo "Digest: sha256:${SHA}"
70	
71	  # Construct GAR destination — anchor is both the package name and the tag
72	  DEST_REF="${_RBGE_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGE_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGE_ENSHRINES_ROOT}/${ANCHOR}:${ANCHOR}"
73	  echo "Dest: ${DEST_REF}"
74	
75	  # Copy upstream to GAR with anchor tag, preserving manifest list
76	  skopeo copy --all \
77	    "docker://${ORIGIN}" \
78	    "docker://${DEST_REF}" \
79	    --dest-creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
80	    || { echo "FATAL: skopeo copy failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }
81	
82	  echo "Slot ${SLOT} enshrined: ${ANCHOR}"
83	
84	  # Accumulate JSON result (no jq dependency — printf safe for sanitized values)
85	  # The "anchor" key carries the full locator (writeback target for RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR).
86	  if [ "${FIRST}" = "true" ]; then
87	    FIRST=false
88	  else
89	    RESULT="${RESULT},"
90	  fi
91	  RESULT="${RESULT}\"slot_${SLOT}\":{\"anchor\":\"${LOCATOR}\",\"origin\":\"${ORIGIN}\",\"digest\":\"sha256:${SHA}\"}"
92	done
93	
94	RESULT="${RESULT}}"
95	
96	echo "=== Writing anchor results ==="
97	echo "${RESULT}"
98	
99	# Write to buildStepOutputs channel
100	mkdir -p /builder/outputs
101	printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output
102	
103	echo "=== Enshrine step complete ==="
104
